blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Sophie. You went upstairs to shower.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Around two. Yes. I was up there — I don't know. Forty minutes? Maybe more.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I'm not implying anything. I'm asking the same questions Davenport is going to ask.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Then yes. I was alone. I showered, I dried my hair, I came back down in clean clothes. That's all.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I saw you come back down. Your hair was damp.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: But the forty minutes before that — no one can account for.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I was in the shower, Dean. That's not a gap. That's just showering.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I know that. I'm saying what it looks like from the outside.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: From the outside it looks like I took a shower on a beach trip. Which is what I did.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: What about you, Dean. The kitchen.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I made two trips. Ice, then drinks. I stood at the counter for a few minutes on my phone — work thing, I was replying to an email. Then I came back out.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Anyone see you in the kitchen either time?
